author: u/composite
title: TIFU by going on a Bumble date with a girl who brought a CLIPBOARD and rated me on a checklist
body: Matched on Bumble with this girl who seemed cool — we both liked Star Wars, hiking, real conversations. She suggested coffee. I said yes.
I showed up at the coffee shop. She was there. Smiling. With a clipboard.
Before I could even order, she opened the clipboard and pulled out a printed, laminated list. Each row had a checkbox. The header said: 'CANDIDATE PRE-SCREENING — ROUND 1.'
She told me — quote — 'Don't worry, this is just my screening process. I do this for every date.'

The questions: salary range. Degree level. Religious affiliation. Why your most recent relationship ended. Whether you want children, and by what year. Each answer, she literally checked a box. With a pen. On a clipboard. In a coffee shop.
After fifteen minutes she said — 'Okay, you're at seven out of twelve. You need nine to advance to Round 2.' She pulled out a SECOND page labeled 'ROUND 2 QUESTIONS.'
I told her I needed the bathroom. There was a back exit. I left.
Three hours later she texted: 'Most candidates run. That's why I prepped Round 2 questions early.'
narration:
So I matched with this girl on Bumble. We had a great text exchange — we both like Star Wars, hiking, real conversations. She suggested coffee. I said yes.

I show up at the coffee shop. She's there. Smiling. With a CLIPBOARD.

I sit down. Before I can even order, she opens this clipboard and pulls out a printed list. Laminated. Each row has a checkbox. The header says — quote — 'CANDIDATE PRE-SCREENING — ROUND 1.'

No joke.

She goes — 'Don't worry. This is just my screening process. I do this for every date.'

The questions. Salary range. Degree level. Religious affiliation. Why your most recent relationship ended. Whether you want children, AND BY WHAT YEAR.

Each answer she literally CHECKS A BOX. With a pen. On a clipboard. In a coffee shop. While I sip a latte.

After fifteen minutes she goes — quote — 'Okay, you're at seven out of twelve. You need nine to advance to Round 2.' She pulls out a SECOND page labeled 'ROUND 2 QUESTIONS.'

I told her I had to use the bathroom. There was a back exit. I left.

Three hours later she texts: 'Most candidates run. That's why I prepped Round 2 questions early.'
